figures.caption: Figure 2: All-incorrect ratio (left two) and hint reliance (right two) over training steps. Both HiLL variants substantially reduce the fraction of degenerate all-incorrect groups compared to GRPO. Transfer weighting (HiLL vs. HiLLw/o TW) keeps hint reliance consistently lower throughout training.
Table 2: Example hints generated by HiLL and HiLLw/o TW on the same question. HiLL provides conceptual strategies while HiLLw/o TW tends to set up the computation directly.
Table 1: Main results on in-distribution and out-of-distribution benchmarks. Best results are in bold and second-best are underlined. HiLL consistenctly outperforms GRPO and hint-based baselines.

Figure 1: Overview of our HiLL framework. Given a question q with an all-incorrect group, the hinterHϕ takes the question, a failed rollout τk, and the reference solution z⋆ as input, and generates M candidate hints. The reasoner πθ re-samples G rollouts under each hinted input q+hj . Each hint is then scored by signal creation (Sec. 4.2) and signal transfer (Sec. 4.3). The best hinted group is selected reasoner GRPO update, while all candidate hints form the group for the hinter GRPO update.
